question.en: In a singly linked list, which description matches the operation «InsertAtBeginning»? (Lec 6 DSA Linked List)

options.A: Removes a node from a given position of the linked-list.
options.B: Attaches a new node at the start of the linked-list.
options.C: Removes the node present at the end of the linked-list.
options.D: Attaches a new node at a specific position in the linked-list.

questions.explanation: Attaches a new node at the start of the linked-list.

question.en: In a binary search tree, values in the left sub-tree of a node with value v are: (Lec 8 DSA BST)

options.A: all greater than v
options.B: all less than v
options.C: unordered relative to v
options.D: always equal to v

questions.explanation: BST property: every value in the left sub-tree is < v.

question.en: To find the minimum in a BST you typically walk: (Lec 8 DSA BST)

options.A: repeatedly to the right child
options.B: repeatedly to the left child
options.C: level-order from the root
options.D: only the root

questions.explanation: Smallest value is at the leftmost node.

question.en: Merge sort primarily follows which approach? (Lec 9 sort)

options.A: Greedy
options.B: Divide and Conquer
options.C: Brute force only
options.D: Dynamic programming only

questions.explanation: Merge sort divides the array, sorts halves, then merges.

question.en: Bubble sort compares which pairs in each inner step? (Lec 9 sort)

options.A: elements far apart (gap > 1)
options.B: adjacent pairs
options.C: only first and last
options.D: random pairs

questions.explanation: It walks through the list comparing adjacent elements and swapping if out of order.

question.en: Worst-case time complexity of bubble sort is typically: (Lec 9 sort)

options.A: O(n)
options.B: O(n log n)
options.C: O(n²)

questions.explanation: Nested loops over the list yield quadratic time in the worst case.

question.en: An undirected graph's adjacency matrix for a simple graph is often: (Lec 7 Graph & Tree)

options.A: asymmetric
options.B: symmetric
options.C: always diagonal only
options.D: always zero

questions.explanation: Edges go both ways, so entries (i,j) and (j,i) match.

question.en: In adjacency list representation, each array entry typically stores: (Lec 7 Graph & Tree)

options.A: only vertex names
options.B: neighbors of that vertex (often as a list)
options.C: the full matrix row
options.D: edge weights only, never vertices

questions.explanation: array[i] lists vertices adjacent to vertex i.
